summary: Add `temper diff <harness>`: re-scan the on-disk harness, compare each artifact's source hash to the surface's import baseline, and report in-sync / drifted / added / removed. Read-only.

per.section: Drift / apply — three states, never two (the hard core)

new.description: A `diff` that loads the surface `Workspace` and re-reads each artifact's `provenance.source_path` from disk, recomputing its SHA-256 and classifying it against the stored `import_hash`: in-sync, drifted (source changed since import), or removed (source gone). A fresh per-kind scan of the harness surfaces added (on disk, absent from the surface). Returns a typed report; renders nothing itself.

edit.description: Add a `Diff { harness_path, into }` subcommand mirroring `Check`'s workspace default; load the surface, run `drift::diff` against the harness path, print the report, and exit 0 (read-only — drift is a report, not a gate).

tests.asserts: `temper diff` over an unchanged, freshly-imported harness reports every artifact in-sync; editing one source file flips it to drifted; a new on-disk artifact shows as added and a deleted source as removed; the command writes nothing and exits 0.

acceptance: `temper diff <harness> --into <ws>` re-reads the on-disk harness, compares each artifact against the imported baseline (`import_hash`), and prints in-sync / drifted / added / removed without writing anything.
notes: First, read-only slice of the three-state drift engine — the real-on-disk vs import-baseline axis only. The surface-edit and post-apply-fingerprint axes arrive with `apply`/`re-add` (both future in the CLI surface). Reuses import's per-kind scan and the per-artifact `provenance.import_hash`; no write-back. Fork-free: `(surface-authority)`/`(yaml-writeback)`/`(workspace-scope)` all RESOLVED.

summary: Dispatch `check` over specs: embed `contracts/spec.toml`, project `ws.specs` via `spec_features`, and run admissibility + `engine::validate` (the advisory `max_lines` clause) over the spec features.

per.section: Worked example: `spec`, temper's own custom kind

gate.reason: the untracked `contracts/spec.toml` is curated human territory (build embeds it via `include_str!` but never writes/adds it); a human must commit it before the gate can embed it in a clean commit

edit.description: Add `BUILTIN_SPEC_CONTRACT = include_str!("../contracts/spec.toml")`; in the `Check` arm project `ws.specs` to spec features via `extract::spec_features`, parse the spec contract, and extend the diagnostic stream with `engine::admissibility` + `engine::validate` over them.

tests.asserts: `temper check` over an imported corpus runs the spec contract — the over-length specs trip `max_lines` at advisory severity and the run still exits 0 absent `--deny-advisories`.

acceptance: `temper check` over an imported corpus runs the spec contract — flagging the over-length specs at advisory severity and exiting 0 (non-blocking) absent `--deny-advisories`.
notes: Build-order: needs SPEC-FEATURES (the extractor) on disk first, plus a human to commit the untracked `contracts/spec.toml`. Runs only spec.toml's active `max_lines` clause; its two commented clauses (decisions-name-alternatives, references-resolve) await `(decision-marker-predicate)` and the references-resolve frontier — both also downstream of that spec.toml commit.
